title: Koi Pond

jokes.text: Oh, why, is there a place closer that sells them?
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Dwight went all the way to NYC for bagels
jokes.punchline: Dwight's oblivious response suggesting NYC is the only place to get bagels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - his literal-mindedness and lack of awareness create humor through deadpan delivery

jokes.text: You owe me one. You all owe me one.
jokes.setup: Dwight brings bagels as a nice gesture
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands everyone owes him a favor
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - can't do anything purely altruistic. The repetition and escalation work well

jokes.text: No. I had a fish stick sandwich. Actually, I had two fish stick sandwiches. My girlfriend didn't want hers, because I guess I'm the only aphrodisiac she needs.
jokes.setup: Simple question about being hungry
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate overshare about his sex life
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael oversharing, but the fish stick aphrodisiac line is absurd enough to work

jokes.text: Fish sticks are not an aphrodisiac. You're thinking of deer penis.
jokes.setup: Michael's fish stick aphrodisiac comment
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact correction with deer penis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment - his encyclopedic knowledge of weird facts delivered deadpan creates perfect absurdist humor

jokes.text: Can't a guy just buy some bagels for his friends so they'll owe him a favor, which he can use to get someone fired who stole a co-manager position from him anymore?
jokes.setup: Dwight talking head about his bagel gesture
jokes.punchline: The escalating reveal of his true manipulative intentions
jokes.explanation: Great structure - starts innocent and builds to reveal Dwight's elaborate scheme. The fake exasperation sells it

jokes.text: Jeez... When did everyone get so cynical?
jokes.setup: Dwight just revealed his manipulative bagel plan
jokes.punchline: He immediately acts like everyone else is the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to the previous joke - Dwight's complete lack of self-awareness is the punchline

jokes.text: Oh, that's right, you're a woman, and you need to refuse food the first time. I'll try again. Please, Pam, reconsider and have a bagel.
jokes.setup: Pam politely declines a bagel
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sexist generalization and awkward persistence
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight inappropriate comment, though not his most original moment

jokes.text: Michael's been trying to get Jim and me to hang out ever since he started dating my mom. I don't know, I really hoped this thing would just die out. But today he's planning a birthday lunch for my mom, and we have to go. No way out.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining the family dynamic
jokes.punchline: Her trapped resignation to the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main cringe scenario effectively, relateable family awkwardness

jokes.text: I've always found beauty in uncommon places. Homeless people. Graffiti photography lets me capture all that.
jokes.setup: Andy showing photography as artistic expression
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of homeless people as beautiful subjects
jokes.explanation: Andy's pretentious artist persona mixed with insensitive comment creates good cringe

jokes.text: There's no better medicine than birthday lunch. It'll cure all of your, 'gee, I don't know if Michael should be dating my mother,' and fixes all occurrences of, 'I don't really see them together.' So open wide, Pam, and take a big ol' spoonful of birthday lunch medicine. Take with food.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to make the lunch less awkward
jokes.punchline: His extended metaphor gets increasingly ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael over-explanation that makes things more awkward. The 'take with food' tag is nice

jokes.text: Yeah, the rest of the story has been censored due to inappropriosity. Because of sex? Kevin! Hey, please, Kevin... You're fired. Sorry. Work with a bunch of idiots.
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate morning story
jokes.punchline: Kevin's blunt question and Michael's overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Kevin's obliviousness meets Michael's embarrassment. The firing threat is absurd

jokes.text: Pam fakes a shipping emergency to avoid lunch, Jim helps cover, and they share a knowing look
jokes.setup: Pam desperately wants to avoid the awkward lunch
jokes.punchline: The visual of their coordinated deception
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing the couple's teamwork against awkwardness

jokes.text: Why did I get in the car? I could have struggled. I have a whistle in my purse. I didn't even blow it.
jokes.setup: Pam stuck in awkward lunch situation
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly dramatic thoughts about escape
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mild regret to kidnapping scenario. The whistle detail is perfect

jokes.text: So a little elbow grease, and she's polished right up, back to her former glory. Feel it against your cheek.
jokes.setup: Andy returning Dwight's favor by cleaning his briefcase
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortably intimate request to feel it
jokes.explanation: Andy's over-the-top politeness creates awkward moment. The cheek suggestion pushes it into cringe territory

jokes.text: You give me a gift--Thank you note. You invite me somewhere--RSVP. You do me a favor--Favor returned. Do not test my politeness.
jokes.setup: Andy's compulsive need to return favors
jokes.punchline: The threatening tone about his politeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - politeness as aggression. The threat about politeness is brilliantly contradictory

jokes.text: Here's a healthy fitness tip. If you clench your buttocks together while walking, you can really take the pressure off your knees. Is that right? Observe. Right? That really works.
jokes.setup: Andy giving fitness advice
jokes.punchline: The visual of him demonstrating weird walking technique
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that would be funnier visually than in transcript form

jokes.text: Do not walk around with your jacket cattywampus.
jokes.setup: Andy's compulsive helpfulness
jokes.punchline: The old-fashioned word 'cattywampus'
jokes.explanation: Andy's vocabulary choice fits his character but not a strong standalone joke

jokes.text: Andy is complicating things. But I'm not worried. This will only up my game.
jokes.setup: Andy competing with Dwight's niceness
jokes.punchline: Dwight treating kindness as warfare strategy
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - turning basic human decency into competitive strategy

jokes.text: Oh, maybe Michael will start dating that too.
jokes.setup: Pam mentions the restaurant being part of her childhood
jokes.punchline: Her bitter joke about Michael dating everything from her past
jokes.explanation: Good bitter humor from Pam showing her frustration with the situation

jokes.text: All I see are boring old tables with no birthday decorations. Oh, wait a second! You mean this one?
jokes.setup: Michael acts like he doesn't see the decorated table
jokes.punchline: His obvious fake surprise at 'discovering' it
jokes.explanation: Michael's attention-seeking behavior, though predictable setup and payoff

jokes.text: The only time you should care about a woman's age is if she is too young for you. And I am not robbing the cradle. If anything, I am robbing the grave.
jokes.setup: Michael defending the age gap with Helene
jokes.punchline: The incredibly inappropriate 'robbing the grave' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Michael's attempt to sound enlightened leads to horrifically inappropriate metaphor. Perfectly crafted misdirection

jokes.text: Michael looks uncomfortable when asked about grandma names, long pause
jokes.setup: Family discussion about grandparent names
jokes.punchline: Michael's visible discomfort with the commitment implications
jokes.explanation: Good visual beat showing Michael's internal conflict about the relationship's future

jokes.text: Nothing. Just life and... Doing things... Before you die. I don't know. Do I really want to go snowboarding? No. But I-- I would like to if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: Michael's increasingly uncomfortable questions about adventure activities
jokes.punchline: His circular, nonsensical reasoning about wanting to want things
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Michael's midlife crisis through confused logic about desires

jokes.text: Gosh, these tacos are awfully complicated to make. I will make everyone's tacos.
jokes.setup: Andy bought lunch for everyone
jokes.punchline: Dwight's competitive need to one-up with taco assembly
jokes.explanation: Shows the escalating favor war between Dwight and Andy, though not exceptional

jokes.text: It's not amazing at all. It's sort of weird that I went and did that. And now your wish won't come true, so that blows.
jokes.setup: Michael explains retrieving a penny from a wishing well for Helene
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-awareness that it was creepy and counterproductive
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Michael moment where he recognizes his own weirdness mid-explanation

jokes.text: Well, that's because you and I have very different taste. And you like lame things. It's a generational sort of gap between us.
jokes.setup: Helene compliments Michael's thoughtful scrapbook gift
jokes.punchline: Michael insults her taste while trying to be self-deprecating
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael foot-in-mouth moment where his insecurity makes him insult someone complimenting him

jokes.text: Home run, Michael. You set the bar so high. That was a bunt.
jokes.setup: Andy compliments Michael's romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-deprecating sports metaphor
jokes.explanation: Decent character interaction showing Michael's insecurity, though not remarkable

jokes.text: Honestly, for a long time I thought I was going to grow old with Holly. And then I met Helene, and she is great. And she already knows and has done everything that I have ever wanted to do, and can tell me about it. But I think I want to do those things myself.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on his relationship with Helene
jokes.punchline: His realization that her experience is actually a problem for him
jokes.explanation: Deep character moment that sets up the breakup - Michael's immaturity disguised as self-awareness

jokes.text: I want you to enjoy that cake because I have something terrible I need to tell you, and I want you to enjoy your cake before I tell you this terrible, terrible thing.
jokes.setup: Michael about to break up with Helene on her birthday
jokes.punchline: His anxiety making the buildup worse than necessary
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup - Michael making the situation worse by over-preparing for bad news

jokes.text: There's another woman, and her name is Italy... and skydiving... and bungee jumping.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain why he's breaking up with Helene
jokes.punchline: His metaphor treating activities as romantic rivals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment where Michael's mixed metaphor creates genuine poetry in its ridiculousness

jokes.text: And you, unfortunately, have already completed that part of your journey down there. It's not my decision. It's mother nature. And mother has very strict rules about... fertility.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he wants kids
jokes.punchline: His awkward euphemism about menopause and fertility
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Michael moment - inappropriate topic handled with maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: What if Dwight dies and I still owe him something? That is a recipe for a ghost.
jokes.setup: Kevin worried about owing Dwight a favor
jokes.punchline: His bizarre logic about ghost creation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - completely wrong but internally consistent. The phrase 'recipe for a ghost' is inspired

jokes.text: Damn it! I am no closer to taking Jim down. Ugh, what a waste of the day. I could have grown poison mushrooms that'd be this high by now! They're mushrooms. They don't get that high.
jokes.setup: Dwight frustrated about his failed scheme
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-correction about mushroom growth
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - his fantasy is interrupted by his own botanical accuracy. Great character consistency

jokes.text: You're bribing me. No, no. No, I am not. Unless you want me to. You want me to? 'Cause I will. I will bribe you. No... your face is saying don't.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to give Pam a raise to improve her mood
jokes.punchline: His confusing negotiation with himself while reading her face
jokes.explanation: Good Michael panic spiral as he realizes he's offering bribes while denying it

jokes.text: Oh, come on, what do you want? What do you want? Do you want a million dollars? Do you want to hit me? You want me to get down on one knee and beg you? I want to hit you. What? I don't-- are you kidding? No. Are you kidding? Ye--no.
jokes.setup: Michael desperately trying to appease angry Pam
jokes.punchline: Pam's simple, direct desire to hit him catches him off guard
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and misdirection - Michael's elaborate offers meet Pam's simple violent desire

jokes.text: Are you scared? Never. About what? A little. What are you talking about? Heard about the punch. What punch? Pam. She's gonna punch the crap out of your face after work.
jokes.setup: Darryl asking if Michael is scared
jokes.punchline: Michael's contradictory responses and growing panic
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Michael's bravado cracking under pressure

jokes.text: And Pam has that crazy pregnancy strength now. I'm getting concerned that you don't seem to understand what's going to happen.
jokes.setup: Darryl warning Michael about the fight
jokes.punchline: The concept of 'crazy pregnancy strength'
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of the fight threat with absurd but believable 'pregnancy strength' concept

jokes.text: But at the same time, she goes crazy. And I do understand her reasoning. I know. I just need some time. Give me some time to think about it. You gotta do something. Give me some time to make a decision. How much time do you need? No more than a week. Maybe two.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for time to decide about getting punched
jokes.punchline: His escalating timeline requests for something happening today
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's tendency to avoid immediate consequences through procrastination

jokes.text: And... The--the power comes from the back foot. So it's all one motion through the body. So there's-- you stay a little low. There's a twist. You keep your shoulder down. You kind of throw the arm.
jokes.setup: Jim giving Pam punching advice
jokes.punchline: The detailed technique instruction for hitting his boss
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's supportive husband role in absurd situation

jokes.text: Am I scared of getting hit in the face? No. Every day, weirdos pay dominatrices hundreds of dollars for that very privilege. I'm scared I'm gonna love it.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about being scared
jokes.punchline: The unexpected twist that he might enjoy being hit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor misdirection - takes expected fear and twists it into unexpected self-discovery

jokes.text: You're just a bunch of voyeurs. Are you eating popcorn? Has almost no calories.
jokes.setup: Michael calling out office watching the fight
jokes.punchline: Stanley's defensive calorie justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Stanley with his food obsession meeting unexpected situation

jokes.text: Okay, just... Don't ever date a member of my family again. Okay. No problem. For the record, your mom came on to me.
jokes.setup: Pam making reasonable request after hitting Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately violating the spirit with unnecessary detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - can't leave well enough alone even after being punched

jokes.text: Holy crap!
jokes.setup: Michael gets punched for the mom comment
jokes.punchline: His shocked reaction to the second, harder punch
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff - his surprise at consequences matching the setup

jokes.text: As I watched Pam's big, strong hand coming toward my face, I saw my entire life flash before my eyes. And guess what. I have four kids. And I have a hover-car and a hover-house. And my wife is a runner, and it shows. And Pam and Jim are my best friends, and our kids play together. And I am happy, I'm rich, and I never die. It doesn't sound like much, but it's enough for me.
jokes.setup: Michael's life flashing before his eyes
jokes.punchline: His idealized future vision with absurd details like hover-cars
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment mixing absurd sci-fi elements with genuine emotional truth about friendship and happiness

jokes.text: Fire Jim. No. I'm cashing in the favor. Fire him! What are you talk... Do it! Promote me. Tri-managers? Forget that. Can I have an office? No. You know what? You can keep your stupid favor. It's worthless. And this is my dinner on your face. I'm keeping it!
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to cash in his favor while Michael has chicken on his face
jokes.punchline: The increasingly desperate negotiation ending with dinner claiming
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the favor system while escalating to absurd claims about dinner ownership

tmdb_overview: Michael falls into koi pond; Pam and Andy make cold calls as weakest salespeople.
